rulings.Case Title: TAMARA AVILA ALVARADO ET AL VS. SALVADOR C. TANDOC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Amended Application And Amended Application For Issuance Of (1) Order For Sale Of A Dwelling And (2) Order To Show Cause Why Order Or Sale Of A Dwelling Should Not Be Made
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 29, 2025. Line 1.2. PLAINTIFFS'Amended Application For Issuance Of (1) Order For Sale Of A Dwelling And (2) Order To Show Cause Why Order Or Sale Of A Dwelling Should Not Be Made is OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service on file. The Court notes that no Proposed Amended Order to Show cause in the form attached as Ex. A to October 9, 2025 ex-parte order has been submitted to the Court for execution. See sections 1 and 3 of October 9, 2025 Ex-parte order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KARENA APPLE FENG VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 29, 2025. Line 5. Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 29, 2025. Line 5. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend for the reasons stated in the moving papers. Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Plaintiff's late-filed opposition is considered. Plaintiff fails to suggest any possible amendments that could cure the issues raised in the moving papers regarding the events that took place in April 2015 (per Plaintiff's complaint in this case) and addressed in 2019 Federal case. The Court notes that Plaintiff filed a non-confidential request for a reasonable accommodation. The request is granted as follows: (1) the motion is being ruling upon on the papers only; there shall be no oral argument; (2) the tentative ruling shall be e-mailed to the Plaintiff (afengre@gmail.com) by the clerk of the Court; (2) Plaintiff is excused from in-person appearance for October 29, 2025 hearing. The request is otherwise denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 29, 2025. Line 8. DEFENDANT ELIZEU XAVIER HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION is DENIED. Prejudgment Claim of Right to Possession form was served with the Summons and Complaint. rulings.Case Title: BV HOUSING PRESERVATION, LP VS. ALISSA THOMAS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 29, 2025. Line 9.1. DEFENDANT ALISSA THOMAS DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, subject to opposition. The Notice to Pay Rent or Quit in 30 Days is fatally defective because it does not comply with SFRO 37.9(c). This defect in the underlying Notice cannot be cured by amending the Complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CENTRAL TOWERS JOINT VENTURE LLC VS. CRISTHIAN TZEK VILLAFANIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That The Truth Of Matters Be Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 29, 2025. Line 10. PLAINTIFF CENTRAL TOWERS JOINT VENTURE LLC Motion For Order That The Truth Of Matters Be Deemed Admitted Hearing required. This is Plaintiff's second motion requesting this relief. At the hearing on the first motion, Plaintiff's counsel, Michael Arikat, represented to the Court that the responses had been received. The moving papers for the instant motion do not acknowledge the previous motion, but simply assert, via the declaration of a different attorney for Plaintiff, Evan J. Wynns, that no responses have been served. Hearing is required to address the contradictory representations made by Plaintiff's various attorneys. Michael Arikat and Evan J. Wynns are both ordered to appear, either in person or via Zoom with video capabilities.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
